title: The First Stars
puzzle: How did the universe go from darkness to blazing stars?
insight: After the Big Bang, gas clouds of hydrogen and helium slowly collapsed under gravity until the first stars ignited and lit the cosmos.
key_ideas: The early universe contained simple elements.
Gravity pulled gas into dense clumps.
Fusion began when pressure and heat became intense enough.
mind_blower: The first stars helped forge heavier elements later used to build planets, oceans, and people.
next_curiosity: If the first stars changed everything, how do astronomers look for traces of them today?
narration_text: How did the universe go from darkness to blazing stars? After the Big Bang, gas clouds of hydrogen and helium slowly collapsed under gravity until the first stars ignited and lit the cosmos. The early universe contained simple elements. Gravity pulled gas into dense clumps. Fusion began when pressure and heat became intense enough. The first stars helped forge heavier elements later used to build planets, oceans, and people. If the first stars changed everything, how do astronomers look for traces of them today?
